nativeRange.habitat: Reef flats, lagoons and seaward reefs from the surface to about 30 meters; tolerates turbid waters subject to freshwater runoff.

decorPreferences: live rock with caves and crevices

diet.notes: In the wild, has been documented feeding on algae, coral polyps, anemones, crustaceans, and worms. In captivity, generally accepts a varied diet of meaty foods (mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, krill, chopped clams, mussels) alongside marine algae (nori, spirulina). Newly acquired specimens have been known to be reluctant feeders; live foods may be needed initially while transitioning to prepared foods. Frequent small feedings (multiple times daily) are generally advised, particularly for new arrivals.

avoidWith: very timid fishes that cannot compete for food

summary: A widespread Indo-Pacific butterflyfish with distinctive criss-cross chevron patterning and a yellow tail bordered by black bands. Generally considered suitable for intermediate aquarists in large, established systems; not reef-safe due to coral and invertebrate predation.
careNotes:
The Vagabond Butterflyfish (Chaetodon vagabundus) is one of the more widespread butterflyfishes, ranging across the Indo-Pacific from the Red Sea and East Africa to the Line and Tuamotu islands, north to southern Japan and south to Lord Howe and the Austral Islands. It inhabits reef flats, lagoons, and seaward reefs from the surface to about 30 meters, and is unusually tolerant of turbid water and freshwater runoff for a butterflyfish.

This species is generally considered not reef-safe. It has been documented feeding on coral polyps (both stony and soft), anemones, tubeworms, sponges, and occasionally Tridacna clam mantles in the wild, and these behaviors typically persist in captivity.

Adult size reports vary somewhat across sources: LiveAquaria and Cincinnati Coral cite a maximum of 9 inches (~23 cm), FishBase records 23 cm TL with a common length of 15 cm, AquariumDomain cites 8 inches (~20 cm), and ReefApp cites 17 cm. A working range of 17–23 cm has been used here.

Minimum tank size recommendations also vary: LiveAquaria and Cincinnati Coral specify 125 gallons (~475 L), AquariumDomain notes 90 gallons (~340 L) for a single specimen rising to 125 gallons for a pair, and ReefApp recommends 500 L (~132 gal). 500 L has been adopted here as a cautious round value, consistent with the species' active swimming behavior and need for ample open space alongside live rock structure.

Temperament is reported inconsistently across sources: LiveAquaria rates it as semi-aggressive (noting it can hold its own against more aggressive tankmates), while ReefApp and AquariumDomain describe it as peaceful with mild conspecific aggression. Semi-aggressive has been used as the middle estimate; in practice it is generally tolerant of most tankmates but may quarrel with similarly-patterned butterflyfishes in tight quarters.

The species is oviparous and forms stable monogamous pairs in the wild, with both members jointly defending a feeding territory. Aquarists are generally advised to acquire a mated pair together rather than introduce a second specimen later. Captive breeding has not been documented.

Water temperature recommendations span 22–29°C across sources; a moderate range of 23–27°C has been used here. Salinity recommendations span 1.020–1.026 SG depending on whether the source is geared toward fish-only or full-reef tanks.

Feeding can be challenging with newly acquired specimens; live foods are commonly used for the first few weeks while transitioning to prepared diets. Smaller specimens may be particularly reluctant feeders.
breedingNotes: Oviparous; forms stable monogamous pairs in the wild, with both pair members jointly defending a feeding territory. Pelagic larvae settle into shallow back-reef habitats. Captive breeding has not been documented and is generally considered not achievable under typical aquarium conditions.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary source for hobby husbandry: 9" max size, 125 gal tank, 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, semi-aggressive, not reef-safe, moderate care.

additional.name: Reef2Reef wiki
additional.notes: Designated secondary returned HTTP 403; could not be retrieved. Replaced with FishBase, ReefApp, and AquariumDomain for cross-verification (recorded below).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Chaetodontidae, Acanthuriformes), max size (23 cm TL), distribution (Indo-Pacific Red Sea to Tuamotu, southern Japan to Lord Howe/Austral), depth (0–30 m), temperature (24.7–29.3°C wild range), diet (omnivorous: algae, coral polyps, crustaceans, worms), and reproduction (oviparous, monogamous pairs).

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (17 cm), temperature (~26°C), salinity (~1.026 SG), tank size (500 L), temperament (peaceful with minor conspecific conflict), and reef-safety (not reef safe).

additional.name: AquariumDomain
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (8"/~20 cm), temperature (72–80°F), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), tank size (90 gal solo / 125 gal pair), omnivore diet, moderate care, peaceful temperament with mild conspecific aggression. Confirmed sandy substrate + live rock decor preference.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; forms stable monogamous pairs in the wild, with both pair members jointly defending a feeding territory. Pelagic larvae settle in shallow back-reef habitats. Not known to reproduce in typical aquarium settings.
